prose.en: Persistently Complete Detection (requirement varies by certification class; see class tailorings and the class-variants payload).

a.statement: Providers with Class A Certifications SHOULD persistently perform vulnerability detection on all information resources that are NOT likely to drift, at least once every 6 months.

b.statement: Providers with Class B Certifications SHOULD persistently perform vulnerability detection on all information resources that are NOT likely to drift, at least once every 6 months.

c.statement: Providers with Class C Certifications SHOULD persistently perform vulnerability detection on all information resources that are NOT likely to drift, at least once every month.

d.statement: Providers with Class D Certifications SHOULD persistently perform vulnerability detection on all information resources that are NOT likely to drift, at least once every month.
